title: Inline image insertion in blog posts, remove login page, fix missing post in admin

requirements.summary: Add inline image insertion toolbar button to blog post create and edit forms, allowing users to upload images and insert them at cursor position in the content textarea, with proper rendering in the detail view
acceptanceCriteria: An 'Insert Image' button is visible in the content editor toolbar on both CreateBlogPostPage and EditBlogPostPage.
Clicking the button opens a file picker or upload dialog for selecting an image.
The selected image is uploaded and an inline image tag/placeholder is inserted at the current cursor position in the content textarea.
When the blog post is viewed on BlogPostDetailPage, inline images appear correctly at the positions they were inserted.
Existing inline image rendering logic in BlogPostDetailPage and imageUtils.ts is preserved and extended to support new insertions.

file_operations.description: Add an 'Insert Image' toolbar button above the content textarea. Use the blob-storage component to handle image uploads via ExternalBlob.fromBytes with upload progress tracking. When an image is uploaded, insert an inline image marker (e.g., {{image:position}}) at the current cursor position in the textarea. Store the uploaded ExternalBlob in the inlineImages array with its position index. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing. Use a file input with accept='image/*' for the file picker.

Add an 'Insert Image' toolbar button above the content textarea, identical to CreateBlogPostPage. Use the blob-storage component to handle image uploads via ExternalBlob.fromBytes with upload progress tracking. When an image is uploaded, insert an inline image marker at the current cursor position and append the ExternalBlob to the existing inlineImages array. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing. Preserve existing inline images when editing.

Extend the existing inline image rendering logic to handle newly inserted inline image markers from the content field. Use the existing imageUtils.ts functions to replace inline image markers with actual image URLs from the inlineImages array. Ensure images render at the correct positions where they were inserted during post creation/editing. Preserve all existing rendering behavior for previously created inline images.

Extend the existing inline image utility functions to support parsing and replacing the new inline image markers (e.g., {{image:position}}) inserted by the editor toolbar. Add a function to replace markers with tags using ExternalBlob.getDirectURL() for each inline image. Preserve all existing utility functions and their behavior for backward compatibility.

requirements.summary: Remove the login/authentication gate from admin pages, making CreateBlogPostPage and AdminPostsPage directly accessible without requiring Internet Identity login
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to the admin page (e.g., /admin or /admin/posts) does not redirect to a login page or show an access-denied screen.
The AdminRoute guard is removed or set to always allow access for admin-facing pages.
The create/edit/delete blog post functionality remains fully operational without authentication.
Internet Identity login/logout in the Navigation bar can remain for other purposes but is not required to access admin pages.

file_operations.description: Remove or bypass the authentication and admin permission checks. Allow all users to access wrapped admin pages by directly rendering the children prop without checking identity or calling canCallerAccessAdminSection(). Optionally remove the entire component if no longer needed, but if kept, ensure it always renders children without any guards.

Remove AdminRoute wrapper from admin-related routes (e.g., /admin, /admin/posts, /admin/posts/create, /admin/posts/edit/:id) so they render directly without authentication guards. Ensure admin pages are accessible to all users without requiring login. Keep the route definitions intact but remove any guard components wrapping them.

Keep the Internet Identity login/logout functionality in the navigation bar for other purposes, but ensure the Admin link is visible and accessible to all users without requiring authentication. Remove any conditional rendering that hides the Admin link based on authentication state.

requirements.summary: Fix the bug where the blog post titled 'Best Ayurvedic Herbs for Glowing Skin' does not appear on the admin page by investigating data fetch, publish state filters, or backend query issues, and ensuring all posts are listed regardless of publish state
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Best Ayurvedic Herbs for Glowing Skin' blog post is visible in the admin posts list.
All blog posts, including unpublished/draft ones, are shown on the admin page.
The backend query used by AdminPostsPage fetches all posts without filtering out drafts or unpublished posts.
No console errors related to missing posts appear when the admin page loads.

file_operations.description: Review the data fetching logic to ensure it calls the correct backend query hook that retrieves all posts (including drafts and unpublished posts). Verify that the hook uses getAllPostsForAdminPage() rather than getPublishedPosts(). Add console logging to debug if posts are being filtered on the frontend. Ensure no local filtering removes unpublished or draft posts from the displayed list. Add error handling and display any fetch errors to the user.

Review the useGetAllPostsForAdminPage() hook (or equivalent) to ensure it correctly calls actor.getAllPostsForAdminPage() and does not apply any frontend filtering that would exclude unpublished or draft posts. Verify the query key and enabled state are correct. Add error logging to capture any issues during the backend call. Ensure the hook returns all posts as received from the backend without modification.
